header.subtitle: Systems Design Police Dept
menu.title: Menu
guide.title: Concept Guide
home.heroSubtitle: The city of Distributia runs on distributed systems. Things keep breaking. Diagnose failures. Design solutions. Solve cases.
chaos.sectionSubtitle: Inject failures, watch the system wobble, then restore stability with targeted fixes.
chaos.title: Chaos Simulator
chaos.subtitle: Introduce faults, observe behavior, then apply fixes to restore service health.
chaos.logsEmpty: No events yet. Activate a fault to begin.
chaos.preset.apiDbCache.name: API + DB + Cache
chaos.preset.apiDbCache.desc: A classic read-heavy stack with cache acceleration and a primary/replica database.
chaos.preset.eventOrders.name: Event-Driven Orders
chaos.preset.eventOrders.desc: Checkout sends work through a queue to background workers and notifications.
chaos.preset.realtimeAnalytics.name: Real-Time Analytics
chaos.preset.realtimeAnalytics.desc: Stream processing feeds a time-series store for dashboards.
chaos.fault.latencySpike.name: Latency Spike
chaos.fault.latencySpike.desc: Upstream calls exceed the p95 budget and slow the critical path.
chaos.fault.packetLoss.name: Packet Loss
chaos.fault.packetLoss.desc: Network drops cause retransmits and timeouts across services.
chaos.fault.nodeCrash.name: Node Crash
chaos.fault.nodeCrash.desc: A core service instance stops responding abruptly.
chaos.fault.dbPrimaryFail.name: DB Primary Failure
chaos.fault.dbPrimaryFail.desc: The primary database goes dark and writes stall.
chaos.fault.replicaLag.name: Replica Lag
chaos.fault.replicaLag.desc: Replicas fall behind, serving stale or inconsistent reads.
chaos.fault.cacheMissStorm.name: Cache Miss Storm
chaos.fault.cacheMissStorm.desc: Hot keys miss, forcing expensive origin reads.
chaos.fault.queueBacklog.name: Queue Backlog
chaos.fault.queueBacklog.desc: Queued work piles up faster than consumers can drain it.
chaos.fix.addRetries.name: Add Retries
chaos.fix.addRetries.desc: Retry transient failures with backoff to reduce error spikes.
chaos.fix.circuitBreaker.name: Enable Circuit Breaker
chaos.fix.circuitBreaker.desc: Fail fast and fall back when a dependency is unhealthy.
chaos.fix.replicaFailover.name: Replica Failover
chaos.fix.replicaFailover.desc: Promote a replica or shift reads to healthy nodes.
chaos.fix.scaleOut.name: Scale Out
chaos.fix.scaleOut.desc: Add capacity to absorb load and reduce queueing.
chaos.fix.cacheWarmup.name: Cache Warmup
chaos.fix.cacheWarmup.desc: Preload hot keys to recover hit rate quickly.
chaos.fix.queueAutoscale.name: Queue Autoscale
chaos.fix.queueAutoscale.desc: Increase consumers to drain backlog faster.
case.locked: Complete the previous case to unlock this one.
rankUp.title: PROMOTED
rankUp.subtitle: You are now
settings.title: Settings
tutorial.step1Body: The symptoms are your first clues. Every case starts with a briefing — read it carefully before touching the diagram.
tutorial.step2Body: Tap the glowing components in the diagram to inspect their logs and evidence. That's where the real clues are.
tutorial.step3Body: Once you know what broke, diagnose the root cause and prescribe a fix in the panel on the right.
